source.title: Staged docs for Award Funding Rollup

observations.statement: Documentation defines the route as POST /api/v2/awards/funding_rollup/ and describes it as returning total transaction obligations plus counts of awarding agencies, funding agencies, and federal accounts for an award.
The docs mark award_id as the required request attribute and also show page, sort, order, and limit in the sample request body even though they are not described as formal attributes.
The documented 200 response body is a single aggregate JSON object with total_transaction_obligated_amount, awarding_agency_count, funding_agency_count, and federal_account_count.
The current raw profile exposes only award_id in its input schema and does not expose page, sort, order, or limit as MCP request fields.
The current raw profile records prior live behavior that unknown award identifiers return HTTP 200 with zero totals instead of an error and that additional body keys are ignored.

The current raw profile also records prior live behavior that award_id accepts strings or integers, trims surrounding whitespace for strings, rejects floats, booleans, null, arrays, and objects with HTTP 422, and can return negative total_transaction_obligated_amount values for some awards.

source.title: Award funding rollup view and validator

observations.statement: Source code decorates the view with validate_post_request([get_internal_or_generated_award_id_model()]), so the endpoint validates only award_id and accepts either an integer internal award id or a text generated award id.
The view's business logic reads request_data['award_id'] and ignores other body keys; if award_id is an integer it queries the award_id column, otherwise it queries generated_unique_award_id.

The SQL sums transaction_obligated_amount from financial_accounts_by_awards rows that are attached to submission windows whose reveal date has passed, counts distinct awarding and funding top-tier agencies, and counts distinct federal accounts by agency_id-main_account_code combinations from treasury appropriation accounts.

source.title: Derived MCP request coverage check for funding rollup

observations.statement: Relative to the staged docs, the current raw profile omits four documented request-body fields from the MCP surface: page, sort, order, and limit.

Those omitted fields are still worth preserving semantically because they appear in the docs and help explain the doc/API contradiction, even though source and prior profile evidence indicate the live service ignores them.

source.title: Live baseline probe for award funding rollup

observations.statement: A live POST with a generated award id returned HTTP 200 JSON on 2026-05-12, confirming the endpoint is currently callable.

The live response matched the documented single-object aggregate shape and returned total_transaction_obligated_amount 2729344672.23 with awarding_agency_count 1, funding_agency_count 1, and federal_account_count 17.
